name: Lineage Cell Therapeutics, Inc.
business: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing cell replacement therapies to treat serious medical conditions. Our most clinically-advanced program to date is OpRegen (RG6501), an allogeneic retinal pigmented epithelial (RPE) cell replacement therapy currently in Phase 2a development under a worldwide collaboration with F. Hoffman-La Roche Ltd. and Genentech, Inc., a member of the Roche Group, for the treatment of geographic atrophy (GA) secondary to dry-AMD.
sellsTo: Patients with geographic atrophy secondary to dry-AMD

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing cell replacement therapies to treat serious medical conditions.
Our board of directors has delegated to its audit committee oversight authority of our information security (including cybersecurity) risk management.

catalysts.event: First milestone payment received from Roche under OpRegen collaboration
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we received the first development milestone for $5 million which was achieved based on manufacturing and clinical advancements related to the OpRegen cell therapy program.

catalysts.event: Initiation of DOSED clinical study for OPC1
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced that we were initiating our DOSED clinical study to evaluate the safety and utility of a novel spinal cord delivery device designed to administer OPC1 to the spinal parenchyma in both subacute and chronic spinal cord injury.

catalysts.event: First chronic SCI patient treated in DOSED study
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, the first chronic SCI participant, with a neurologically complete SCI injury was treated in the DOSED study at UC San Diego Health, and the novel delivery system successfully administered a one-time injection of OPC1.

catalysts.event: Second clinical site in DOSED study opened for enrollment
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, our second clinical site in the DOSED study, Rancho Research Institute, in conjunction with Rancho Los Amigos National Rehabilitation Center, opened for enrollment.

catalysts.event: Launch of Type 1 Diabetes cell therapy research initiative
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we announced the launch of a new cell therapy research initiative focused on addressing the issue of large-scale production of undifferentiated pluripotent cells, which if successful could support the production of islet cells to support a potential treatment of Type 1 Diabetes.

catalysts.event: Collaboration with William Demant Invest 2 Aps for ReSonance program
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, we announced that we entered into a research collaboration agreement with William Demant Invest 2 Aps (WDI) to advance the preclinical development of ReSonance for the treatment of hearing loss.

catalysts.event: Receipt of novel iPSC line from Factor Biosciences
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we announced the receipt of the novel iPSC line with Factor.

catalysts.event: Termination of development under CRT License Agreement
catalysts.sentence: Effective June 30, 2025, Lineage elected to cease development and commercialization of all products under the CRT License Agreement.
In November 2025, the Company elected to withdraw its application for the CIRM Grant.
In May 2024, we established a new Services Agreement with Genentech, a member of the Roche Group, to support ongoing development of OpRegen.

tamClaims.claim: 18.34 million individuals in the U.S. 40 years and older with early-stage AMD and 1.49 million with late-stage AMD in 2019
tamClaims.sentence: According to a 2022 report in JAMA Ophthalmology, 18.34 million individuals in the U.S. 40 years and older (11.64%) were living with early-stage AMD and 1.49 million (0.94%) were living with late-stage AMD in 2019.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 18,000 new spinal cord injuries annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 18,000 new spinal cord injuries annually in the U.S. (NSCIC SCI Facts and Figures at a Glance (2025)), and there are currently no FDA-approved drugs specifically for the treatment of SCI.
